devanagari: स्वविरहार्तगोपगोपीनां सान्त्वनाय भगवतोद्धवस्य प्रस्थापनम् नन्दोद्धवसंवादश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । वृष्णीनां प्रवरो मन्त्री कृष्णस्य दयितः सख...
english_devnagari: svavirahārtagopagopīnāṃ sāntvanāya bhagavatoddhavasya prasthāpanam nandoddhavasaṃvādaśca śrīśuka uvāca vṛṣṇīnāṃ pravaro mantrī kṛṣṇasya dayitaḥ sakhā śiṣyo bṛhaspateḥ sākṣāduddhavo buddhisattamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Uddhava was the foremost counsellor of the Vṛṣṇis and the utmost beloved friend of Kṛṣṇa. He was a direct disciple of Bṛhaspati (the preceptor of gods and a founder of the political science) and was most intelligent.

devanagari: तमाह भगवान्प्रेष्ठं भक्तमेकान्तिनं क्वचित् । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं प्रपन्नार्तिहरो हरिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāha bhagavānpreṣṭhaṃ bhaktamekāntinaṃ kvacit gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṃ prapannārtiharo hariḥ
translation: On one occasion, holding Uddhava’s hand in his own, Lord Hari, the destroyer of miseries of those who seek asylum in him, spoke to him—his most beloved votary of unflinching devotion.

devanagari: गच्छोद्धव व्रजं सौम्य पित्रोर्नौ प्रीतिमावह । गोपीनां मद्वियोगाधिं मत्सन्देशैर्विमोचय ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: gacchoddhava vrajaṃ saumya pitrornau prītimāvaha gopīnāṃ madviyogādhiṃ matsandeśairvimocaya
translation: “Oh gentle-natured Uddhava! Please do go to Vraja. Bring felicity to our[1] parents (Nanda and Yaśodā) by communicating my message to them. Please relieve the (mental) agony of separation from me of the Gopīs by conveying my special message to them.

devanagari: ता मन्मनस्का मत्प्राणा मदर्थे त्यक्तदैहिकाः । मामेव दयितं प्रेष्ठमात्मानं मनसा गताः । ये त्यक्तलोकधर्माश्च मदर्थे तान्बिभर्म्यहम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tā manmanaskā matprāṇā madarthe tyaktadaihikāḥ māmeva dayitaṃ preṣṭhamātmānaṃ manasā gatāḥ ye tyaktalokadharmāśca madarthe tānbibharmyaham
translation:
They have entirely devoted their heart and soul to me and they regard me as their very life. For my sake they have given up their husbands and sons—nay their bodily requirements[2] as well.
4(A). They are united with me in mind regarding me as their beloved-most, the climax of their love and their Lord.
I always support and give happiness to those who renounce all pleasures and interests and means to obtain them here and hereafter, and have over-ruled popular moral conventions for my sake.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
4(A). Some editions (e.g. Yande’s Bombay edition) add this line here:
māmeva dayitaṃ preṣṭham ātmānam manasā gatāḥ /

devanagari: मयि ताः प्रेयसां प्रेष्ठे दूरस्थे गोकुलस्त्रियः । स्मरन्त्योऽङ्ग विमुह्यन्ति विरहौत्कण्ठ्यविह्वलाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: mayi tāḥ preyasāṃ preṣṭhe dūrasthe gokulastriyaḥ smarantyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Myself, the foremost of their beloved objects, being away at a distant place, the women of Gokula always brood over me and being overwhelmed with deep anxiety and grief caused by separation from me, they have become oblivious to everything else, dear friend!

devanagari: धारयन्त्यतिकृच्छ्रेण प्रायः प्राणान्कथञ्चन । प्रत्यागमनसन्देशैर्बल्लव्यो मे मदात्मिकाः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhārayantyatikṛcchreṇa prāyaḥ prāṇānkathañcana pratyāgamanasandeśairballavyo me madātmikāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]
[nominative dual from √ dhṛ ], [nominative plural from √ dhṛ ], [vocative dual from √ dhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ dhṛ ], [accusative dual from √ dhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ dhṛ ]
[nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: It is due to my message assuring them of my return to visit them that the cowherd women whose heart and soul is absorbed in me are somehow managing to stay alive with great difficulty”.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्त उद्धवो राजन्सन्देशं भर्तुरादृतः । आदाय रथमारुह्य प्रययौ नन्दगोकुलम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityukta uddhavo rājansandeśaṃ bharturādṛtaḥ ādāya rathamāruhya prayayau nandagokulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh king! When thus instructed Uddhava received with respect and devotion the message of his Lord. Getting into his chariot, he drove fast to Nanda’s Gokula.

devanagari: प्राप्तो नन्दव्रजं श्रीमान्निम्लोचति विभावसौ । छन्नयानः प्रविशतां पशूनां खुररेणुभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: prāpto nandavrajaṃ śrīmānnimlocati vibhāvasau channayānaḥ praviśatāṃ paśūnāṃ khurareṇubhiḥ
translation: While the sun was setting and his chariot was obscured with the thick cloud of dust raised by the hoofs of the cattle that were returning home, the blessed Uddhava reached Nanda’s Vraja (and could contact Nanda without Gopīs being aware of his arrival).

devanagari: वासितार्थेऽभियुध्यद्भिर्नादितं शुष्मिभिर्वृषैः । धावन्तीभिश्च वास्राभिरूधोभारैः स्ववत्सकान् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsitārthe dhāvantībhiśca vāsrābhirūdhobhāraiḥ svavatsakān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nad ], [accusative single from √ nad ]

translation: It (the Gokula) was noisy to the roaring of maddened bulls (in rut) fighting with one another for cows in heat, and by rushing of cows with heavy udders to meet their calves.

devanagari: इतस्ततो विलङ्घद्भिर्गोवत्सैर्मण्डितं सितैः । गोदोहशब्दाभिरवं वेणूनां निःस्वनेन च ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: itastato vilaṅghadbhirgovatsairmaṇḍitaṃ sitaiḥ godohaśabdābhiravaṃ veṇūnāṃ niḥsvanena ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ ]
[nominative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ maṇḍ ], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ ]
[instrumental plural from √ si class 5 verb], [instrumental plural from √ si class 9 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ si class 5 verb], [instrumental plural from √ si class 9 verb]

translation: It appeared beautiful with snow-white calves (briskly) frisking and capering here and there and with the musical notes of flutes and (whistle-like) sounds while milking the cows.

devanagari: गायन्तीभिश्च कर्माणि शुभानि बलकृष्णयोः । स्वलङ्कृताभिर्गोपीभिर्गोपैश्च सुविराजितम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: gāyantībhiśca karmāṇi śubhāni balakṛṣṇayoḥ svalaṅkṛtābhirgopībhirgopaiśca suvirājitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]

translation: The Gokula appeared most resplendent with richly adorned Gopīs and Gopas, the former glorifying in songs the auspicious deeds of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: अग्न्यर्कातिथिगोविप्र पितृदेवार्चनान्वितैः । धूपदीपैश्च माल्यैश्च गोपावासैर्मनोरमम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: agnyarkātithigovipra pitṛdevārcanānvitaiḥ dhūpadīpaiśca mālyaiśca gopāvāsairmanoramam
translation: It was charming with the habitations of the cowherds where the sacred fire, the sun-God, guests, cows, Brāhmaṇas, ancestors and gods were being worshipped with flowers, lights and fragrant frank-incense burnt before them (OR and which were beautified by garlands, lamps and burning frankincense).

devanagari: सर्वतः पुष्पितवनं द्विजालिकुलनादितम् । हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णैः पद्मषण्डैश्च मण्डितम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvataḥ puṣpitavanaṃ dvijālikulanāditam haṃsakāraṇḍavākīrṇaiḥ padmaṣaṇḍaiśca maṇḍitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nad ], [accusative single from √ nad ]
[accusative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ ]
[nominative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ maṇḍ ], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ ]

translation: On all sides, it abounded in groves in full blossom and resounded with the sweet warblings and notes of birds and hummings of bees. It was beautified with ponds full of lotus-beds teeming with swans and Kāraṇḍavas (a species of ducks).

devanagari: तमागतं समागम्य कृष्णस्यानुचरं प्रियम् । नन्दः प्रीतः परिष्वज्य वासुदेवधियार्चयत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāgataṃ samāgamya kṛṣṇasyānucaraṃ priyam nandaḥ prītaḥ pariṣvajya vāsudevadhiyārcayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [vocative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [vocative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]

translation: Seeing (and receiving) Uddhava, the beloved follower of kṛṣṇa, who had arrived there, Nanda was exceedingly delighted. He embraced him and worshipped him (with due formalities and respect) regarding him to be Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) himself.

devanagari: भोजितं परमान्नेन संविष्टं कशिपौ सुखम् । गतश्रमं पर्यपृच्छत्पादसंवाहनादिभिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhojitaṃ paramānnena saṃviṣṭaṃ kaśipau sukham gataśramaṃ paryapṛcchatpādasaṃvāhanādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhuj ], [accusative single from √ bhuj ]

translation: When he was served with rich dishes and was comfortably resting on a bed, and had been relieved of his fatigue by shampooing and such other services, Nanda enquired of him.

devanagari: कच्चिदङ्ग महाभाग सखा नः शूरनन्दनः । आस्ते कुशल्यपत्याद्यैर्युक्तो मुक्तः सुहृद्व्रतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccidaṅga mahābhāga sakhā naḥ śūranandanaḥ āste kuśalyapatyādyairyukto muktaḥ suhṛdvrataḥ
translation: “Oh Highly blessed dear Uddhava! Is our friend Vasudeva, the delight (or son) of Śūra happy and doing well, as he is now free (from imprisonment) united with his sons, wives and others and is surrounded by his friends and well- wishers?

devanagari: दिष्ट्या कंसो हतः पापः सानुगः स्वेन पाप्मना । साधूनां धर्मशीलानां यदूनां द्वेष्टि यः सदा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā kaṃso hataḥ pāpaḥ sānugaḥ svena pāpmanā sādhūnāṃ dharmaśīlānāṃ yadūnāṃ dveṣṭi yaḥ sadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: The wicked Kaṃsa who always hated the righteous and religious-minded Yadus, has fortunately been killed by his own sins along with his followers.

devanagari: अपि स्मरति नः कृष्णो मातरं सुहृदः सखीन् । गोपान्व्रजं चात्मनाथं गावो वृन्दावनं गिरिम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: api smarati naḥ kṛṣṇo mātaraṃ suhṛdaḥ sakhīn gopānvrajaṃ cātmanāthaṃ gāvo vṛndāvanaṃ girim
translation: Does Kṛṣṇa remember us, his mother Yaśodā, his friends, well-wishers and other Gopas, Vraja of which he was the protector, cows, Vṛndāvana and mount Govardhana!

devanagari: अप्यायास्यति गोविन्दः स्वजनान्सकृदीक्षितुम् । तर्हि द्रक्ष्याम तद्वक्त्रं सुनसं सुस्मितेक्षणम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: apyāyāsyati govindaḥ svajanānsakṛdīkṣitum tarhi drakṣyāma tadvaktraṃ sunasaṃ susmitekṣaṇam
translation: Will Kṛṣṇa, the Protector of cows, come at least once, to see his friends, kith and kin (hereof)? Then alone we shall be able to see his charming countenance with a shapely nose, happy smiles and (loving) glances.

devanagari: दावाग्नेर्वातवर्षाच्च वृषसर्पाच्च रक्षिताः । दुरत्ययेभ्यो मृत्युभ्यः कृष्णेन सुमहात्मना ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dāvāgnervātavarṣācca vṛṣasarpācca rakṣitāḥ duratyayebhyo mṛtyubhyaḥ kṛṣṇena sumahātmanā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ rakṣ ], [vocative plural from √ rakṣ ]

[nominative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ rakṣ ], [vocative plural from √ rakṣ ], [accusative plural from √ rakṣ ]

translation: We have been protected from the forest conflagration, stormy winds and driving showers, the demon in the bullock form (Ariṣṭa), the serpent (Kāliya) and from perils and fatal accidents which were difficult to be avoided, by the highly noble-souled Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: स्मरतां कृष्णवीर्याणि लीलापाङ्गनिरीक्षितम् । हसितं भाषितं चाङ्ग सर्वा नः शिथिलाः क्रियाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: smaratāṃ kṛṣṇavīryāṇi līlāpāṅganirīkṣitam hasitaṃ bhāṣitaṃ cāṅga sarvā naḥ śithilāḥ kriyāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: Dear Uddhava! When we remember the heroic exploits of Kṛṣṇa, his sportive glances, his smiles and talks, all our actions become slackened.

devanagari: सरिच्छैलवनोद्देशान्मुकुन्दपदभूषितान् । आक्रीडानीक्ष्यमाणानां मनो याति तदात्मताम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: saricchailavanoddeśānmukundapadabhūṣitān ākrīḍānīkṣyamāṇānāṃ mano yāti tadātmatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Even as we see the play-grounds, the river Yamunā, hills, forests and groves adorned with the foot-prints of Kṛṣṇa, our mind becomes absorbed in him.

devanagari: मन्ये कृष्णं च रामं च प्राप्ताविह सुरोत्तमौ । सुराणां महदर्थाय गर्गस्य वचनं यथा ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: manye kṛṣṇaṃ ca rāmaṃ ca prāptāviha surottamau surāṇāṃ mahadarthāya gargasya vacanaṃ yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: As observed by Garga, I too regard that Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa are the most prominent divinities who have descended on this earth for accomplishing some important mission of gods.

devanagari: कंसं नागायुतप्राणं मल्लौ गजपतिं यथा । अवधिष्टां लीलयैव पशूनिव मृगाधिपः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsaṃ nāgāyutaprāṇaṃ mallau gajapatiṃ yathā avadhiṣṭāṃ līlayaiva paśūniva mṛgādhipaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Both of them easily killed, as in a sport, Kaṃsa who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, the two wrestlers (Cāṇūra and Muṣṭika) and the king of elephants. Kuvalayāpīḍa.

devanagari: तालत्रयं महासारं धनुर्यष्टिमिवेभराट् । बभञ्जैकेन हस्तेन सप्ताहमदधाद्गिरिम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tālatrayaṃ mahāsāraṃ dhanuryaṣṭimivebharāṭ babhañjaikena hastena saptāhamadadhādgirim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Like a king of elephants breaking down a sugarcane, Kṛṣṇa snapped the formidable bow three tālas (i.e. twentyseven spans) in length. And on one hand, he held up the mount Govardhana for a period of seven days.

devanagari: प्रलम्बो धेनुकोऽरिष्टस्तृणावर्तो बकादयः । दैत्याः सुरासुरजितो हता येनेह लीलया ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: pralambo dhenuko daityāḥ surāsurajito hatā yeneha līlayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: By him were killed as in a sport demons like Pralamba, Dhenuka, Ariṣṭa, Tṛṇāvarta, Baka and others who have vanquished gods and asuras.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य नन्दः कृष्णानुरक्तधीः । अत्युत्कण्ठोऽभवत्तूष्णीं प्रेमप्रसरविह्वलः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti saṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya nandaḥ kṛṣṇānuraktadhīḥ atyutkaṇṭho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Recollecting graphically again and again the sports and exploits of Kṛṣṇa, Nanda whose mind and intellect were deeply attached to Kṛṣṇa lapsed into silence, as he was overwhelmed with extreme longing and (felt choked up) with a flood of affection.

devanagari: यशोदा वर्ण्यमानानि पुत्रस्य चरितानि च । शृण्वन्त्यश्रूण्यवास्राक्षीत्स्नेहस्नुतपयोधरा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodā varṇyamānāni putrasya caritāni ca śṛṇvantyaśrūṇyavāsrākṣītsnehasnutapayodharā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb]

[nominative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: While Yaśodā was listening to the description of the exploits of her sons, she shed tears profusely and her breasts overflowed with milk through maternal affection.

devanagari: तयोरित्थं भगवति कृष्णे नन्दयशोदयोः । वीक्ष्यानुरागं परमं नन्दमाहोद्धवो मुदा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoritthaṃ bhagavati kṛṣṇe nandayaśodayoḥ vīkṣyānurāgaṃ paramaṃ nandamāhoddhavo mudā
translation: Noticing such a supreme affection of Nanda and Yaśodā towards Lord Kṛṣṇa, Uddhava spoke with great joy to Nanda.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । युवां श्लाघ्यतमौ नूनं देहिनामिह मानद । नारायणेऽखिलगुरौ यत्कृता मतिरीदृशी ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca yuvāṃ ślāghyatamau nūnaṃ dehināmiha mānada nārāyaṇe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation:
Uddhava said:
“Oh courteous bestower of honours![3] Both of you are indeed the most praiseworthy among embodied beings in this world, inasmuch as you cherish such a deep love for Nārāyaṇa (Kṛṣṇa) who is the Creator of all beings.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
You gave us the honour of belonging to the fraternity of devotees of the Lord from Nārada down to a resident of Gokula—VT.

devanagari: एतौ हि विश्वस्य च बीजयोनी रामो मुकुन्दः पुरुषः प्रधानम् । अन्वीय भूतेषु विलक्षणस्य ज्ञानस्य चेशात इमौ पुराणौ ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: etau hi viśvasya ca bījayonī rāmo mukundaḥ puruṣaḥ pradhānam anvīya bhūteṣu vilakṣaṇasya jñānasya ceśāta imau purāṇau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third dual], [perfect middle third dual]

translation: For these two—Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa—are the primary (efficient and material) cause of the Universe; they are the Puruṣa (spirit) and Pradhāna (Primordial matter). Permeating all beings, these two Ancient(beginningless) Lords control varieties of consciousness.

devanagari: यस्मिन्जनः प्राणवियोगकाले क्षणं समावेश्य मनोऽविशुद्धम् । निर्हृत्य कर्माशयमाशु याति परां गतिं ब्रह्ममयोऽर्कवर्णः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminjanaḥ prāṇaviyogakāle kṣaṇaṃ samāveśya mano nirhṛtya karmāśayamāśu yāti parāṃ gatiṃ brahmamayo
translation: If at the time of death, a person concentrates his mind in him at least for a moment, he reduces to ashes all his (accumulated) karmas and (realizing the nature of his self) becomes identical with Brahman, (and attaining a form constituted of pure sattva) acquires the refulgence of the Sun, and instantly reaches the highest goal.

devanagari: तस्मिन्भवन्तावखिलात्महेतौ नारायणे कारणमर्त्यमूर्तौ । भावं विधत्तां नितरां महात्मन्किं वावशिष्टं युवयोः सुकृत्यम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminbhavantāvakhilātmahetau nārāyaṇe kāraṇamartyamūrtau bhāvaṃ vidhattāṃ nitarāṃ mahātmankiṃ vāvaśiṣṭaṃ yuvayoḥ sukṛtyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]

translation: Both of you cherish utmost devotion unto him (Kṛṣṇa) who is the veritable Lord Nārāyaṇa himself, the cause (source) of all beings, and who has assumed a human form for some divine purpose. Oh noble-souled Nanda! What meritorious acts are yet to be achieved by you both!

devanagari: आगमिष्यत्यदीर्घेण कालेन व्रजमच्युतः । प्रियं विधास्यते पित्रोर्भगवान्सात्वतां पतिः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: āgamiṣyatyadīrgheṇa kālena vrajamacyutaḥ priyaṃ vidhāsyate pitrorbhagavānsātvatāṃ patiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [locative single from √ iṣ class 4 verb]

translation: At no distant date, the immutable Lord Kṛṣṇa will visit Vraja again. The glorious of the Sātvatas will accomplish whatever delights his parents.

devanagari: हत्वा कंसं रङ्गमध्ये प्रतीपं सर्वसात्वताम् । यदाह वः समागत्य कृष्णः सत्यं करोति तत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: hatvā kaṃsaṃ raṅgamadhye pratīpaṃ sarvasātvatām yadāha vaḥ samāgatya kṛṣṇaḥ satyaṃ karoti tat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ van class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ van class 8 verb]

translation: Whatever Kṛṣṇa has promised you after slaying Kaṃsa, the enemy of Sātvatas, in the arena[4] he will accomplish it for you.

devanagari: मा खिद्यतं महाभागौ द्रक्ष्यथः कृष्णमन्तिके । अन्तर्हृदि स भूतानामास्ते ज्योतिरिवैधसि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: mā khidyataṃ mahābhāgau drakṣyathaḥ kṛṣṇamantike antarhṛdi sa bhūtānāmāste jyotirivaidhasi
translation: Please do not get depressed, Oh highly fortunate ones! You shall see Kṛṣṇa by your side. Just as the fire (latently) exists in fuel, he abides in the hearts of all beings.

devanagari: न ह्यस्यास्ति प्रियः कश्चिन्नाप्रियो वास्त्यमानिनः । नोत्तमो नाधमो वापि समानस्यासमोऽपि वा ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyasyāsti priyaḥ kaścinnāpriyo vāstyamāninaḥ nottamo nādhamo vāpi samānasyāsamo
translation: (Please do not entertain doubt how he will leave his ḍear parents and come to you). None is extremely beloved of or disliked by him, for he is above attachments. None is superior or inferior or unequal to him as he is the same to all.[5]

devanagari: न माता न पिता तस्य न भार्या न सुतादयः । नात्मीयो न परश्चापि न देहो जन्म एव च ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: na mātā na pitā tasya na bhāryā na sutādayaḥ nātmīyo na paraścāpi na deho janma eva ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ bhṛ ]

translation: He has no mother, no father, no wife, no children or other relatives. He has none who is his own or who is his non-self. He has no corporeal form at all.

devanagari: न चास्य कर्म वा लोके सदसन्मिश्रयोनिषु । क्रीडार्थं सोऽपि साधूनां परित्राणाय कल्पते ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: na cāsya karma vā loke sadasanmiśrayoniṣu krīḍārthaṃ so
translation: He has no bound of Karma. But out of sport and for the protection of the righteous, he incarnates in good (eg. Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, bad (Fish, Boar) and mixed (e.g. Man-lion) types of species.)[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Incarnates among gods, humans and sub-human beings—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति भजते निर्गुणो गुणान् । क्रीडन्नतीतोऽपि गुणैः सृजत्यवन्हन्त्यजः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti bhajate nirguṇo guṇān krīḍannatīto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 1 verb]

translation: Though this unborn Lord is transcendental to and hence unaffected by guṇas, he assumes all the guṇas such as the sattva, rajas and tamas. It is out of sportiveness that he creates, maintains and destroys the universe by these means.

devanagari: यथा भ्रमरिकादृष्ट्या भ्राम्यतीव महीयते । चित्ते कर्तरि तत्रात्मा कर्तेवाहंधिया स्मृतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā bhramarikādṛṣṭyā bhrāmyatīva mahīyate citte kartari tatrātmā kartevāhaṃdhiyā smṛtaḥ
translation: Just as the whole earth appears rotating to an eye that is rolling, the Soul is regarded as the Agent through the influence of ahaṃkāra (Ego) while it is actually the mind that is active.[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Not traced in Padaratnāvalī’s Text.

devanagari: युवयोरेव नैवायमात्मजो भगवान्हरिः । सर्वेषामात्मजो ह्यात्मा पिता माता स ईश्वरः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvayoreva naivāyamātmajo bhagavānhariḥ sarveṣāmātmajo hyātmā pitā mātā sa īśvaraḥ
translation: This glorious Lord Hari is not merely a son of you both, but is the Sun, Soul, father, mother and the Almighty Ruler of all.

devanagari: दृष्टं श्रुतं भूतभवद्भविष्यत् । स्थास्नुश्चरिष्णुर्महदल्पकं च । विनाच्युताद्वस्तुतरां न वाच्यं । स एव सर्वं परमात्मभूतः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭaṃ śrutaṃ bhūtabhavadbhaviṣyat sthāsnuścariṣṇurmahadalpakaṃ ca vinācyutādvastutarāṃ na vācyaṃ sa eva sarvaṃ paramātmabhūtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac ], [accusative single from √ vac ]

translation: Without the presence of and apart from the Lord, there is nothing which is seen or heard of, has happened in the past or present or can happen in future: nothing immobile or mobile, great or small is different from or without the presence of the Lord. For only he is everything and real.

devanagari: एवं निशा सा ब्रुवतोर्व्यतीता नन्दस्य कृष्णानुचरस्य राजन् । गोप्यः समुत्थाय निरूप्य दीपान्वास्तून्समभ्यर्च्य दौधीन्यमन्थुन् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ niśā sā bruvatorvyatītā nandasya kṛṣṇānucarasya rājan gopyaḥ samutthāya nirūpya dīpānvāstūnsamabhyarcya daudhīnyamanthun

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: In this way, while Nanda and Kṛṣṇa’s follower (Uddhava) were talking, the whole night passed away (without their being aware of it). (At dawn) the cowherd women got up, lighted the lamps, duly worshipped the presiding deities of their home e.g. the threshold, and began to churn their curds.

devanagari: ता दीपदीप्तैर्मणिभिर्विरेजू रज्जूर्विकर्षद्भुजकङ्कणस्रजः । चलन्नितम्बस्तनहारकुण्डल त्विषत्कपोलारुणकुङ्कुमाननाः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tā dīpadīptairmaṇibhirvirejū rajjūrvikarṣadbhujakaṅkaṇasrajaḥ calannitambastanahārakuṇḍala tviṣatkapolāruṇakuṅkumānanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛṣ class 1 verb]

translation: The Gopīs appeared splendid with their jewels shining refulgent in the light of lamps, as they pulled to and fro the cords of the churning-rod, with (jingling) rows of bangles on their hands, with their big hips, heavy breasts and pearl necklaces moving (in a rhythmic motion), with their cheeks refulgent with the radiance of the rocking ear-rings (while churning), and with their countenance decked with red saffron.

devanagari: उद्गायतीनामरविन्दलोचनं व्रजाङ्गनानां दिवमस्पृशद्ध्वनिः । दध्नश्च निर्मन्थनशब्दमिश्रितो निरस्यते येन दिशाममङ्गलम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: udgāyatīnāmaravindalocanaṃ vrajāṅganānāṃ divamaspṛśaddhvaniḥ dadhnaśca nirmanthanaśabdamiśrito nirasyate yena diśāmamaṅgalam
translation: The sound of the singing damsels of Vraja glorifying loudly in songs the deeds of the lotus-eyed God (Kṛṣṇa) mixed with the (gurgling) sound of the churning of the curds, filled the heaven and thereby whatever was inauspicious in all directions was dispelled.

devanagari: भगवत्युदिते सूर्ये नन्दद्वारि व्रजौकसः । दृष्ट्वा रथं शातकौम्भं कस्यायमिति चाब्रुवन् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavatyudite sūrye nandadvāri vrajaukasaḥ dṛṣṭvā rathaṃ śātakaumbhaṃ kasyāyamiti cābruvan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb]
[locative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [locative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

[nominative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [locative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb], [locative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

[nominative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ sūr class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ sūr class 10 verb]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single]
[nominative single from √ nand class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nand class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nand class 1 verb]

translation: When the sun rose, and the Gopīs of Vraja saw a gold-plated chariot at the door of Nanda, they enquired whose chariot it was.

devanagari: अक्रूर आगतः किं वा यः कंसस्यार्थसाधकः । येन नीतो मधुपुरीं कृष्णः कमललोचनः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: akrūra āgataḥ kiṃ vā yaḥ kaṃsasyārthasādhakaḥ yena nīto madhupurīṃ kṛṣṇaḥ kamalalocanaḥ
translation: Has Akrūra, the accomplice of Kaṃsa who carried away lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa to Mathura and thus accomplished Kaṃsa’s purpose, come again?

devanagari: किं साधयिष्यत्यस्माभिर्भर्तुः प्रीतस्य निष्कृतिम् । ततः स्त्रीणां वदन्तीनामुद्धवोऽगात्कृताह्निकः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ sādhayiṣyatyasmābhirbhartuḥ prītasya niṣkṛtim tataḥ strīṇāṃ vadantīnāmuddhavo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sidh ], [vocative dual from √ sidh ], [accusative dual from √ sidh ], [locative single from √ sidh ]
[genitive single from √ prī class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[genitive single from √ prī class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation:
Is he going to bring about the gratification of his dead master by offering us i.e. our bodies or flesh as piṇḍa (offering to the deceased) to him. While the women were talking thus, Uddhava who had finished his bath and morning (religious) duties, arrived there.
[back to top]
